- 1+ years of supply chain experience
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent
jobs.description:
o Provides Logistics support across major operations company-wide (Perishables Purchasing)
o Contributes to development of the organization’s overall supply and logistics strategy.
o Supports various launch activities by performing services including:
 Attends and participates in regional, national and inter-team meetings
 Maintaining OneNote updates (SOP’s)
 Forecasting - Logistics
o Identifies, evaluates and capitalizes on opportunities to reduce costs by improving buying patterns and supply channels through collaboration with purchasing
o Accurately manages email flow, maintains good records of communications and provides documentation as required to support inquires and help with issue resolution
o Compiles data, ensures accuracy and performs in-depth analysis as needed
o Analyzes and interprets data results and communicates findings to the team
o Communicates all relevant information and keeps everyone on the same page at all times; fosters collaboration with the group to lower cost of goods to WFM
o Assists with the development of new reporting tools to be used by buying teams (global and regional, to support purchasing operational needs and executive decision-making)
o Prepares materials and supports buyer communication as needed
o Monitors the functional activity processes and outcomes; recommends areas for continuous improvement
o With oversight, performs the truck forecasting tasks, particularly for holiday planning
o Assists with managing transportation carriers
o Supports team leadership by communicating truck planning issues, interpreting weekly reports and resolving arrival conflicts
o Communicates, represents, and upholds WFM’s core values and business philosophy to Team Members and vendor/supply/carrier partners
o Provides data and works as liaison for WFM Purchasing team
o Monitors the trucking and delivery schedules to proactively identify potential issues and conflicts for all regions
o Works with trucking, delivery, storage, and other stakeholders to resolve issues.
o Reviews periodic reports for patterns, trends, and systemic issues; communicating improvement recommendations to Senior Team Leader
o Drives improvement in key performance metrics, such as order fill rate, on-time delivery, inventory turns, service levels at shelf, returns, and inventory levels versus budget.
o Tracks and reports on key metrics on a regular basis.
o Responsible for daily tracking of load status while in transit, understanding and documenting dwell time at pick up and delivery locations.
o Ensures optimal use of processes and policies to enhance efficiency and effectiveness of the Transportation Management System (TMS)
o Maintains complete and accurate records of all transactions and projections.
o Assist with planning and execution of Transportation Procurement RFPs

o Skills: Advanced knowledge of inventory management, carrier relations, ordering and disposition processes and standards
o Advanced proficiency with standard ordering, delivery, trucking and transportation practices and procedures.
o Excellent mathematical skills
o Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel and Word.
o Strong desire to enable others to achieve success
o Comfortable with ongoing shifts in demands and priorities
o Clarifies and handles multiple concurrent and diverse activities
o Excellent client service skills
o Conveys and or consults on issues of moderate to significant complexity
o Regularly interacts with colleagues or customers up to the senior leadership level
o Provides process and policy advice to Team Members and clients/customers
o Receives general direction, work may be completed without pre-established procedures
o Resolves complex issues escalated from TM’s
o Provides direction and coaching for TM’s
o Applies analytical skills to evaluate and interprets complex situations
o Presents problem analyses and recommends solutions in a creative and logical manner
o Handles a diverse range of issues requiring evaluation of a variety of factors, including business trends
o Possesses deep understanding of the WFM organizations structure, hierarchy and approval levels
o Understands and influences the types and timing of various departmental deadlines and events
o Understands how Logistics decision and recommendations may impact third-party business and operations


Required Education/Experience: BA/BS degree and 2-4 years relevant experience OR equivalent combinations of education and relevant experience

- Bachelor's degree in BI, finance, engineering, statistics, computer science, mathematics, finance or equivalent quantitative field

jobs.description:
At Amazon, the Leadership Principle “Strive to be Earth’s Best Employer” challenges us to create “a safer, more productive, higher performing, more diverse, and more just work environment,” and reinforces that nothing is more important than the safety and well-being of our teams. At Workplace Health and Safety (WHS) we strive to do just this. We work tirelessly to ensure we have the appropriate strategy, technology, and attitude to be successful. We also understand that people are at the center of safety and look to invest in talent that shares in our passion for making our employees and partners lives better.

The WHS Data team is hiring a Business Intelligence Engineer (BIE). The Data team spans the Business Intelligence (BI) and Data Engineering (DE) functional areas, and is responsible with providing all reporting solutions required by the WHS Organization to drive workplace safety and regulatory compliance worldwide. This BIE role will primarily support the data and reporting needs for the Business Unit (BU) BI team in the WHS Organization. This role regularly requires close engagement with senior leaders and principal PMs to generate insights and deep dives into core metrics that directly impact organizational strategic decisions and priorities. The role requires high proficiency in complex SQL scripting, often joining together multiple types of data sets that range from normalized summaries to raw information spanning many diverse sources. The role will need to have a high bar for ownership and work autonomously to create high-quality products for the customer. The role is required to communicate project roadmap, prioritization, and release notes of new products with customer and stakeholder groups including leadership.

Key job responsibilities
-Contribute to the design, implementation, and delivery of BI solutions for complex and ambiguous problems
-Conduct deep dive analyses of business problem statements and formulate conclusions and recommendations to leadership
-Identify opportunities and key criteria to drive analytical reporting and business strategy
-Produce written recommendations and insights for key stakeholders that will help shape effective metric development and reporting
-Simplify and automate reporting, audits, and other data-driven activities
-Partner with Data Engineering to enhance data infrastructure, data availability, and broad access to customer insights
-Develop and drive best practices in data integrity, consistency, analysis, validations, and documentation
-Learn new technology and techniques to meaningfully support product and process innovation

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of technical recruiting in high volume and high hiring bar environment experience
- Experience building candidate sourcing and closing strategies
jobs.description:
The Finance and Global Business Services Recruiting team is seeking a Sourcing Recruiter to leverage their sourcing and strategy development skills to deliver for and provide guidance to our FGBS hiring managers and leaders. The recruiter will be well-versed in the recruiting lifecycle and candidate generation.

This recruiter will work with a team of peer recruiters to execute strategies for identifying and engaging with finance talent. They will contribute to a collaborative team environment and a strong service-oriented culture that ensures delivery of efficient, effective, quality driven service to internal customers and meets customer service level agreements.

The ideal candidate must be able to successfully manage, prioritize and close searches against a timeline and have experience setting benchmarks, metrics, and understand how to prioritize to hit all customer headcount targets. They thrive in an innovative, fast-paced environment, can roll up their sleeves, work hard, have fun, and get the job done. The best candidates will draw on extensive knowledge of talent acquisition and functional areas of the business, forging dynamic relationships with business leaders, hiring managers and HR business partners. They have a reputation for being exceptional at candidate generation, organization, and strong verbal and written communication skills. The recruiter will have an ability to prioritize their time, demonstrate push-back and a sense of urgency, and understand the value of the role they play, and in providing amazing customer service.

Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities
- Partner with hiring teams to execute effective sourcing and assessment approaches, managing candidate pipelines while developing a solid understanding of talent attraction and customer needs.
- Recruit passive candidates using a variety of sourcing techniques, and assess candidates against Amazon's Leadership Principles to ensure quality of hire beyond surface-level qualifications.
- Build and maintain a network of potential candidates through proactive market research and ongoing relationship management; conduct thorough candidate interviews and work toward improving offer-to-interview ratios over time.
- Contribute ideas and feedback related to recruitment processes, supporting the implementation of standard and improved sourcing practices that align with Amazon's hiring standards.
- Maintain clear and organized recruiting plans with defined deliverables and timelines, tracking progress and communicating updates to stakeholders.
- Screen, interview, and prepare candidate slates within appropriate and consistent timelines, operating within the framework of the position specification.
- Participate in special projects and recruiting initiatives, including evaluation of interviewing best practices, utilization of internal talent pipelines, and support for key openings across assigned business areas.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- High school or equivalent diploma
- 1+ years of Microsoft Office products and applications experience
- Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
- certification from Amazon's Mechatronics and Robotics Apprenticeship Program, or 2+ years of automated conveyor systems and controls experience
- 2+ years of conducting predictive and preventative maintenance procedures experience
- 2+ years of reading blueprints and electrical schematics experience
- 2+ years of demonstrating safety standards experience
- 2+ years of troubleshooting and diagnostics of material handling equipment (MHE) experience
jobs.description:
About Maintenance at Amazon

Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. Reliability Maintenance & Engineering (RME) are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it all happen. We drive continuous improvement and maintain all of the Robotics and Material Handling Equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us on our journey!

About the Role

As a Mechatronics & Robotics Technician, you will use your skills to support the Operations Maintenance team in repairing and maintaining material handling equipment and pneumatic systems throughout the building. You will have the opportunity to install, maintain, and repair automated packaging and distribution equipment. In addition to developing your skills, you will mentor junior technicians to grow in their roles.

What Do We Offer?

Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there is more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!

Here is what you can expect with the Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) selection process for Mechatronics & Robotics Technicians: (Timeframe: Approximately 30-days)

Step 1: Congratulations! You successfully submitted your application!

Step 2: Amazon Recruiting will review your application and invite the most qualified candidate to complete a pre-employment assessment.

Step 3: If you meet the role qualifications, you will receive an invitation from Amazon Recruiting (do-not-reply@criteriacorp.com) to begin your Criteria assessment. You have 5 days to complete.

Step 4: Once you successfully meet the pre-employment assessment requirements, you will be invited to complete a video interview. Your video interview invitation will be from HireVue (interviews@hirevue.com) and you will have 7 days to record your interview.

Step 5: Your video interview will be reviewed by the Amazon Hiring Team and you will be notified of next steps via email within 5 business days.


Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
- You will promote a safe working environment by following all safety procedures
- You will complete preventative maintenance routines with proper documentation
- You will troubleshoot electrical and mechanical problems related to all Material Handling Equipment (MHE) which includes belts, motors, photo-eyes, relays, and more
- You will maintain a positive working relationship across all of the Amazon Operations facility
- You must be able to move up to 49lbs, stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, climb ladders and gangways safely, and without limitation, as well as perform regular bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching both below the waist and above the head

Please note that while we strive to accommodate employees' preferences for shift assignments, due to operational requirements and scheduling constraints, we cannot guarantee a specific shift assignment.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of technical product or program management experience
- 10+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
- 5+ years of software development experience
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
- Bachelor's degree
jobs.description:
Fitness and Fandom builds customer experiences that connect customers to their passions through relevant, timely, and delightful shopping experiences. Our fandom program creates cohesive experiences where sports fans feel understood by Amazon, celebrating every moment, emotion and milestone from big games and playoffs to trades and drops, helping customers quickly discover and buy licensed franchise merchandise across Sports, Entertainment, and Music.

We are looking for a Principal TPM who is a true sports obsessive, someone who follows teams, leagues, and storylines closely and can translate fan moments into predictable program delivery through crisp technical plans, execution mechanisms, and cross org coordination.

As Principal TPM for Fitness and Fandom Customer Experiences, you will own end to end program delivery for franchise based shopping experiences and the technical capabilities that power them: scalable content pipelines, customer experience modules, experimentation, measurement, and integration across various surfaces. You will partner with Engineering, Science, Design, and business teams across Sports and Outdoors to understand customer and selling partner needs, then drive program development with dedicated engineering, science and design partners and senior leadership.

This role is successful through strong partnership: Business PMs, Marketing, and BD own program strategy, partnerships, and go to market. Engineering and Science own architecture and implementation. This TPM owns execution strategy, technical program mechanisms, dependency management, risk management, and predictable delivery.

You are naturally curious, data driven and entrepreneurial as you take end to end ownership for program execution. You are the kind of leader who anticipates bottlenecks, deftly navigates ambiguity, makes informed trade off decisions and skillfully balances business needs versus technical constraints. You have exceptional written and verbal communication skills that help you build strong partnerships with colleagues and influence senior leaders.

If you enjoy cutting through ambiguity, challenging traditional approaches, eliminating complexity and innovating on behalf of customers, come join us!

Key job responsibilities
• Own end to end program delivery for customer experiences across licensed sports (fandom) and fitness, driving predictable launches through scalable content engines powered by LLMs and generative image models

• Establish and run program mechanisms including weekly reviews, dependency tracking, launch readiness reviews, and WBR/QBR inputs that drive continuous improvement

• Deliver on VP and Director level commitments by owning quarterly business review goals with direct accountability, leveraging data to define success metrics and track performance

• Manage complex cross org dependencies across partner teams including Brand Stores, Ads, Design, Marketing, Personalization, Search, and Browse

• Coordinate AI and automation delivery with strong governance including evaluation plans for LLM powered content, human in the loop review, and quality standards for licensed content

• Anticipate and remove blockers by identifying risks early, driving tradeoffs, and keeping teams focused on critical path to business outcomes

• Improve engineering velocity by standardizing playbooks and repeatable launch patterns for tentpole events such as playoffs, finals, and season openers

• Mentor TPMs and raise the bar on program management craft across the organization

jobs.company_name: Amazon Web Services Colombia S.A.S. - E40
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about solving technical challenges and helping people? Do you thrive in a dynamic, high-impact environment? Join Amazon's IT Services team as an ITS Support Engineer and be part of the engine that powers Amazon’s seamless operations. We're seeking customer-focused, innovative problem-solvers who excel in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. As a part of our IT Services team, you'll have the opportunity to make a direct impact on Amazon's corporate global workforce, making sure their laptops, servers, and websites work seamlessly so they can build the technology that businesses worldwide rely on.


As an ITS Support Engineer, you'll be the go-to knowledge expert for Amazon's corporate employees’, providing seamless end-to-end services and frictionless on-site technical support ensuring productivity remains secure and uninterrupted. Amazon’s ITS Support Engineers are dedicated, dependable and customer focused, owning the overall IT experience. They guarantee our employees have the tools and resources they need to be successful, innovative and efficient.

ITS Support Engineers have an advanced understanding of IT standards, systems and equipment deployed throughout Amazon. They can work independently or collaborate with partner teams and contractors managing projects while maintaining a high level of productivity to meet goals. Quickly adapting to new processes and procedures, they act as a mentor and main partner for escalations within the team.

Your day will be filled with exciting challenges and opportunities to showcase your technical expertise. You’ll engage with Amazonians across various teams, owning and recognizing their unique IT needs and provide personalized high-quality support. You'll be a trusted advisor, a problem-solver, taking initiative to diagnose, troubleshoot and resolve a wide variety of specialized hardware and software issues implementing successful solutions. Our Engineers guide and empower technical and non-technical Amazonians through the ever-evolving digital landscape implementing solutions that fit their needs.



This role will be based in Sao Paulo, Brazil




Key job responsibilities

Deliver on-site, high quality, hands-on support diagnosing, troubleshooting and resolving issues successfully, evaluating log files to determine the health of systems, software and hardware
Provide on-call support for high severity incidents and act as an escalation point of contact for team
Assist with activities to triage and escalate system or network outages to reduce downtime
Understand and execute change management activities in a high availability environment
Participate with partner teams and vendors on continuous improvement projects, defining requirements and managing execution to deliver operational excellence and value
Effectively manage and oversee IT asset inventories
Participate in hiring efforts, training and developing peers and new team members as a mentor, seeking and leveraging diverse perspectives in all activities
Continuously expand skills, learning the latest technologies and maintaining knowledge of IT policies to provide technically accurate solutions

A day in the life
If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we’d still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skill-sets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!

Amazon offers a full range of benefits that support you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment.





About the team
Our IT Services team is a dynamic and diverse group of passionate problem-solvers who thrive on challenges and continuously push the boundaries of innovation. We foster an inclusive and collaborative environment where every voice is heard, and every idea is valued. Join us and be part of a team that truly makes a difference in the lives of Amazonians worldwide.

Inclusive Team Culture: Here at Amazon, it's in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship and Career Growth: We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. We embrace curiosity and continuous learning, empowering you to grow both personally and professionally through mentorship, knowledge-sharing, and career-advancing resources, helping you develop into a better-rounded professional.

If you're ready to join a team of passionate problem-solvers and make a real impact, apply now to become an ITS Support Engineer at Amazon!

- Experience in influencer, affiliate marketing

jobs.description:
This role owns the execution for Whole Foods Market’s influencer program, including managing creator relationships, writing influencer briefs, and managing contracts. Additionally, this role will own our supplier collaboration requests, building out our intake process, manage deliverable tracking, and provide content feedback.


Key job responsibilities
• Creator Relationship Management – Build and maintain relationships with brand advocates.
• Brief Framework Development – Write influencer briefs to ensure high quality of content across all influencers.
• Supplier collaboration – Inspect and recommend changes to our supplier collaboration requests, building a robust content arm for social that also supports our suppliers.

A day in the life
You'll spend your day building relationships with influencers and suppliers across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube. Responsible for sourcing new talent, coordinating partnerships, and ensuring content aligns with Whole Foods' social strategy.

About the team

jobs.description:
This role is the analytical engine of Whole Foods Market's social and influencer program, owning our reporting infrastructure and translating complex data into clear, actionable insights. The Performance Manager will build and maintain our reporting platform, lead monthly performance reporting, and serve as the strategic bridge between data and creative, helping the team understand what's working, what's not, and how to evolve our content approach accordingly. A strong analytical thinker with a storyteller's instinct, this person turns numbers into narratives that drive decisions.

Key job responsibilities
Reporting Platform Ownership – Own and manage WFM's social and influencer reporting platform end-to-end, ensuring data integrity, accessibility, and scalability across the team.
Monthly Performance Reporting – Lead the development and delivery of monthly performance reports, synthesizing data across platforms and programs into clear executive-ready summaries.
Creative Insights & Optimization – Translate video view data and content performance metrics into actionable recommendations that inform and evolve our creative strategy across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube.
Data Storytelling – Build compelling data narratives that connect performance trends to business outcomes, helping stakeholders understand the "so what" behind the numbers.
Cross-Functional Partnership – Collaborate with the Influencer, Community, and Content teams to ensure reporting frameworks reflect program goals and surface insights that drive strategy.


A day in the life
You'll spend your day living in the data, pulling platform analytics, building dashboards, and identifying the trends that matter most. You'll translate video view patterns and engagement signals into clear creative recommendations, partnering closely with the Influencer and Community Specialists to connect performance to execution. At month's end, you'll own the reporting package that goes to leadership, telling the story of what our content is doing and where we should go next.

About the team
You'll join Whole Foods Market's Social Media & Influencer Marketing team as Performance Manager, reporting directly to the Head of Owned & Influencer, and partnering daily with the Influencer, Community, and Content teams to ensure data drives every creative decision we make.

jobs.description:
Application deadline: Apr 30, 2026

About Maintenance at Amazon

Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. Reliability Maintenance & Engineering (RME) are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it all happen. We drive continuous improvement and maintain all of the Robotics and Material Handling Equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us on our journey!

About the Role

As a Mechatronics & Robotics Technician, you will use your skills to support the Operations Maintenance team in repairing and maintaining material handling equipment and pneumatic systems throughout the building. You will have the opportunity to install, maintain, and repair automated packaging and distribution equipment. In addition to developing your skills, you will mentor junior technicians to grow in their roles.

What Do We Offer?

Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there is more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!

Here is what you can expect with the Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) selection process for Mechatronics & Robotics Technicians: (Timeframe: Approximately 30-days)

Step 1: Congratulations! You successfully submitted your application!

Step 2: Amazon Recruiting will review your application and invite the most qualified candidate to complete a pre-employment assessment.

Step 3: If you meet the role qualifications, you will receive an invitation from Amazon Recruiting (do-not-reply@criteriacorp.com) to begin your Criteria assessment. You have 5 days to complete.

Step 4: Once you successfully meet the pre-employment assessment requirements, you will be invited to complete a video interview. Your video interview invitation will be from HireVue (interviews@hirevue.com) and you will have 7 days to record your interview.

Step 5: Your video interview will be reviewed by the Amazon Hiring Team and you will be notified of next steps via email within 5 business days.


Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
- You will promote a safe working environment by following all safety procedures
- You will complete preventative maintenance routines with proper documentation
- You will troubleshoot electrical and mechanical problems related to all Material Handling Equipment (MHE) which includes belts, motors, photo-eyes, relays, and more
- You will maintain a positive working relationship across all of the Amazon Operations facility
- You must be able to move up to 49lbs, stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, climb ladders and gangways safely, and without limitation, as well as perform regular bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching both below the waist and above the head

Please note that while we strive to accommodate employees' preferences for shift assignments, due to operational requirements and scheduling constraints, we cannot guarantee a specific shift assignment.

- 2+ years of demonstrating safety standards experience
- 1+ years of troubleshooting and diagnostics of material handling equipment (MHE) experience
jobs.description:
About Maintenance at Amazon

Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. Reliability Maintenance & Engineering (RME) are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it all happen. We drive continuous improvement, and maintain all of the Robotics and Material Handling Equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us, on our journey!

About the Role

As a Mechatronics & Robotics Technician, you will use your skills to support the Operations Maintenance team in repairing and maintaining material handling equipment and pneumatic systems throughout the building. You will have the opportunity to install, maintain and repair automated packaging and distribution equipment. In addition to developing your skills, you will mentor junior technicians to grow in their roles.

What Do We Offer?

Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there is more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!


Here is what you can expect with the Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) selection process for Mechatronics & Robotics Technicians: (Timeframe: Approximately 30-days)

Step 1: Congratulations! You successfully submitted your application!

Step 2: Amazon Recruiting will review your application and invite the most qualified candidate to complete a pre-employment assessment.

Step 3: If you meet the role qualifications, you will receive an invitation from Amazon Recruiting (do-not-reply@criteriacorp.com) to begin your Criteria assessment. You have 5 days to complete.

Step 4: Once you successfully meet the pre-employment assessment requirements, you will be invited to complete a video interview. Your video interview invitation will be from HireVue (interviews@hirevue.com) and you will have 7 days to record your interview.

Step 5: Your video interview will be reviewed by the Amazon Hiring Team and you will be notified of next steps via email within 5 business days.

Key job responsibilities
Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
- Promote a safe working environment by following all safety procedures
- Complete preventative maintenance routines with proper documentation
- Troubleshoot electrical and mechanical problems related to all Material Handling Equipment (MHE) which includes belts, motors, photo-eyes, relays, and more
- Maintain a positive working relationship across all of the Amazon Operations facility
- Track and store department inventory
- Move up to 49lbs as well as stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, with or without reasonable accommodation

Please note that while we strive to accommodate employees' preferences for shift assignments, due to operational requirements and scheduling constraints, we cannot guarantee a specific shift assignment.

The following are typical shifts in the Mechatronics & Robotics Technician role:

Front Half Day Shift:
Schedule: 4 days on, 3 days off. The days can be set according to your operational needs, a common pattern is working Sunday to Wednesday.
Hours: Typically, this shift might start in the morning and end in the late afternoon. For a 10-hour shift, this could be 6:00 AM to 4:30 PM, with a half-hour break included.

Back Half Day Shift:
Schedule: 4 days on, 3 days off. Often, this shift could be Wednesday to Saturday to complement the Front Half Day Shift.
Hours: Like the Front Half Day shift, this shift would also occur during daytime hours. Depending on your downtime windows, a typical schedule might be from 7:00 AM to 5:30 PM, with a half-hour break.

Front Half Night Shift:
Schedule: 4 days on, 3 days off. The days can be arranged as per your needs, such as Sunday to Wednesday.
Hours: This shift would occur during the night. For example, it could run from 6:00 PM to 4:30 AM, with a half-hour break.

Back Half Night Shift:
Schedule: 4 days on, 3 days off. To complement the Front Half Night Shift, this might run from Wednesday to Saturday.
Hours: Like the Front Half Night shift, this shift would take place during the night. Depending on your downtime windows, a typical schedule might be from 7:00 PM to 5:30 AM, with a half-hour break.

At Amazon, we continuously strive to make sure our opportunities are open to all. We treat all job applications equally, and are always delighted to welcome a new Amazonian. Apply to join our close-knit team and your Amazon RME adventure will begin from day one. Where it goes and what it looks like is up to you!
